models.generation_context: 2nd-gen Trax, introduced for 2024 MY; carryover for 2026 with minor pricing/feature updates
models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover SUV positioned as one of Chevrolet's most affordable vehicles. Single powertrain (1.2L turbo I3 with 6-speed automatic) and FWD-only across all five trims; differences are cosmetic and feature-based.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles so 2026 Trax not yet rated; Consumer Reports has not yet published 2026 predicted reliability. Earlier 2nd-gen Trax (2024-2025) received generally positive owner feedback.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Trax's high value proposition, modern styling, generous standard tech (wireless CarPlay/Android Auto, large touchscreen on upper trims), and refined ride. Common critiques are modest 137-hp powertrain, no available AWD, and noticeable interior plastics.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the smooth ride, roomy interior for the segment, design, and overall value. Common complaints are limited acceleration at highway speeds, occasional infotainment glitches, and absence of rear cupholders.

trims.notes: Base LS trim has 16-inch steel wheels with covers standard; 17-inch alloy wheels noted in step-up trim documentation but availability on LS via Convenience Package per dealer source. NHTSA gave 2026 Trax 4 stars overall (passenger-side small overlap deficient). IIHS 2024-2026 testing: Moderate Overlap Front Poor, so no IIHS Top Safety Pick award. 0-60 estimate from independent press tests (~8.6 sec).

notable_other: RS sport exterior styling and badging

changes: RS sport exterior styling and badging
Machined 17-inch alloy wheels (vs steel with covers on LS)
Black exterior trim accents
Same powertrain, dimensions, safety and 8-inch screen as LS

trims.notes: 1RS adds sport styling at modest price step; equipment otherwise mirrors LS.
LT is the volume-shopper trim and the cheapest with the 11-inch HD screen.

notable_other: Heated front seats and heated steering wheel
Blind-spot monitoring with rear cross-traffic alert

changes: Adds adaptive cruise control, blind-spot monitoring, rear cross-traffic alert
Adds heated front seats, heated steering wheel, power driver's seat
Adds wireless phone charging
Adds RS-specific 19-inch alloy wheels and black contrast roof
11-inch HD touchscreen and 8-inch driver display (vs LS 8-inch / 3.5-inch)
Synthetic leather (leatherette) seating surfaces

trims.notes: 2RS shares trim_family imagery with 1RS but is the better-equipped sport line with leatherette and heating.

notable_other: Active-themed cloth interior with unique stitching

trims.notes: ACTIV emphasizes outdoorsy styling but remains FWD-only with the same 1.2T powertrain (no off-road mechanicals beyond drive-mode selector).

models.notes: Trax is FWD-only with a single 1.2L turbo I3 / 6AT powertrain across the lineup. 1RS and 2RS share the 'rs' trim_family; LS, LT and ACTIV are their own families. IIHS Moderate Overlap Front rated Poor (2024-2026 testing) so no Top Safety Pick award. NHTSA awarded 4-star overall (passenger small overlap rated as 'Marginal' contributed). Image URLs point to chevrolet.com vehicle pages (consumer pages do not expose direct asset URLs to WebFetch) — flagged needs_scraping for Phase 4 follow-up CDN extraction.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Trailblazer (revived nameplate), introduced 2021; updated for 2026 with reshaped LED lighting and new 11-inch infotainment standard on most trims
models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover slotted above Trax with available AWD. Offers a 1.2L turbo I3 base engine (LS/LT FWD) and a stronger 1.3L turbo I3 standard on RS/ACTIV and optional on lower trims, paired with a CVT (FWD) or 9-speed automatic (AWD).

reliability.summary: No JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted reliability published for 2026 MY at research time. Prior years showed average reliability per CR.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Trailblazer's flexibility (available AWD, generous cargo, large standard touchscreen), youthful styling, and segment-low pricing. Common critiques are the loud-at-load three-cylinder engines and Chevy's continued reliance on a CVT on FWD trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise styling, available AWD capability, and cargo flexibility. Complaints center on three-cylinder noise/vibration and modest acceleration.

trims.notes: LS has 1.2T standard with CVT/FWD. 1.3T available as option. NHTSA 5-star carryover from 2025 (current gen unchanged structurally). IIHS testing 2024-2026 shows Good ratings but full award page not yet posted for 2026 — listed as null pending confirmation.

notable_other: Heated front seats and remote start added

trims.notes: AWD bumps drivetrain to 9-speed automatic and 1.3T standard at extra cost.

notable_other: Evotex (synthetic leather) seating with red trim
Heated steering wheel and front seats

trims.notes: RS is the sport-styled top-end gas trim; 1.3T standard with FWD/CVT. AWD switches to 9-speed automatic. EPA values cited for FWD configuration.

notable_other: All-season/all-terrain tires on 17-inch high-gloss black wheels
Blacked-out exterior accents and unique fascia with skid-plate look

trims.notes: ACTIV with AWD has ~8.0 in ground clearance vs 7.5 in for FWD; AWD switches to 9-speed automatic transmission.

models.notes: Trailblazer pricing reflects 2026 lineup with LS, LT, RS, ACTIV. 1.2T standard on LS/LT FWD; 1.3T standard on RS/ACTIV and optional on LS/LT. AWD model uses 9-speed automatic; FWD uses CVT. IIHS 2026 page does not show a Top Safety Pick award. NHTSA 5-star carries over from prior 2025 rating (no structural changes for 2026). All image URLs flagged needs_scraping for Phase 4 follow-up.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Equinox, fully redesigned for 2025; carries over to 2026 with minor changes (drive mode selector standard)
models.model_summary: Compact crossover with a single 1.5L turbo I4 powertrain across all trims. FWD with CVT or AWD with 8-speed automatic. Three personality-driven trims for 2026: value LT, sporty RS, and outdoorsy ACTIV.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (Chevrolet Equinox received compact-SUV segment award in 2026 VDS)
reliability.summary: Chevrolet Equinox received a JD Power 2026 Vehicle Dependability Study compact-SUV segment award (one of four GM model-level wins). Note that VDS measures 3-year-old (2023 MY) vehicles, so the award reflects the prior-generation Equinox, not the all-new 2025/2026 redesign. Chevrolet brand ranked #3 mass market at 178 PP100 (industry avg 204). However, Consumer Reports 2026 specifically flagged the current Chevrolet Equinox as having 'well-below-average reliability' (Chevrolet brand 42/100, rank 17).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers like the bold new exterior, large 11.3-inch standard touchscreen, generous standard ADAS (Chevy Safety Assist plus adaptive cruise on every trim) and competitive pricing. Common critiques include modest 175-hp output relative to size and an interior with hard-plastic surfaces in lower trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the refreshed styling, value, and large screen. Complaints include CVT drone under load and limited rear visibility from new bodywork.

notable_other: Standard adaptive cruise control on all trims
Chevy Safety Assist suite with blind-spot monitoring standard
Heated steering wheel and heated front seats standard on LT

trims.notes: LT is the volume base trim. IIHS 2025-2026 testing shows mostly Good/Acceptable but Equinox is not on the 2026 TSP list yet. NHTSA 2026 rating not yet posted at research time. Heated seats and heated steering wheel are unusually standard at this trim level.

changes: Adds AWD with 8-speed automatic (vs FWD/CVT)
Bumps torque to 203 lb-ft (vs 184 lb-ft FWD)
Larger 15.6-gal fuel tank
Fuel economy 25/29/26 (vs 26/29/27 FWD)
Otherwise identical equipment to LT FWD

trims.notes: AWD bumps torque and changes transmission. EPA verified separately from FWD.

notable_other: Sport-themed RS exterior treatment with blacked-out trim
Leatherette seating with red contrast stitching

trims.notes: RS is FWD by default; AWD bumps price ~$2,000 and adds 8AT. Powertrain unchanged.

notable_other: Off-road-themed ACTIV exterior with skid-plate look
Sueded microfiber-style seat inserts with copper accents
Heated and ventilated front seats available

trims.notes: ACTIV is AWD-standard with 8-speed automatic. New for 2025, carries over for 2026.

models.notes: Equinox lineup simplified for 2026 to three personalities (LT, RS, ACTIV) with FWD/AWD options. ACTIV is AWD-standard. IIHS 2026 individual scores good but no TSP/TSP+ designation; NHTSA 2026 rating not yet posted at research time. All images flagged needs_scraping for Phase 4.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Equinox EV on GM Ultium platform, introduced 2024; carryover for 2026 with new LT1 base trim and pricing reductions
models.model_summary: All-electric Ultium-platform compact SUV marketed separately from gas Equinox. Available with single-motor FWD (220 hp) or dual-motor AWD (288/300 hp). All trims share an 85-kWh battery and CCS1 charge port.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles so 2026 Equinox EV not yet rated. Consumer Reports has not yet published a 2026 predicted reliability score, though early Ultium-platform vehicles (LYRIQ, Blazer EV) had software-related issues.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rate the Equinox EV as the segment value leader for range-per-dollar (319 mi FWD for under $35k pre-incentive). Praise centers on the large 17.7-inch touchscreen, modern Ultium underpinnings, and generous standard equipment. Critiques cite occasional Ultium software glitches and a relatively slow 150-kW peak DC fast charging speed.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise range, value, and tech. Common complaints are occasional software/charging app issues, slow public DC charging, and CCS1 port without native NACS for 2026.

notable_other: Google Built-In with Maps, Assistant, Play Store (no CarPlay/Android Auto)
One-pedal driving with Regen on Demand paddle

trims.notes: LT 1 is the new-for-2026 lower-priced base trim (~$5k less than 2025 LT). Single 180 kW motor / FWD. Google Built-In replaces Apple CarPlay/Android Auto on Ultium models. 2026 still uses CCS1 (NACS adapter available). EV mirroring: MPGe values 117/100/108 populated into city/highway/combined per spec §3.6 v1.1. 5-star NHTSA carries from 2025 testing of same platform.

changes: Adds Evotex (synthetic leather) seating
Adds HD Surround Vision 360-degree camera
Adds hands-free power liftgate
Adds bright roof rails and style upgrades
Same 180 kW motor / FWD powertrain and 319-mile range as LT 1

trims.notes: LT 2 is the mid-trim FWD step-up. AWD is a separate trim selection.

notable_other: Sport-inspired RS styling: black emblems, black mirrors, black 21-inch wheels

trims.notes: RS FWD shares battery and motor with LT 1/LT 2 but with sport-themed styling. AWD is a separate $4-5k upcharge across LT 2/RS lines.

changes: Adds dual-motor AWD (288 hp / 333 lb-ft) vs single-motor FWD (220 hp)
Range drops to 307 mi (vs 319 mi FWD) but acceleration improves to ~6.4 sec 0-60
Adds Evotex seating, HD Surround Vision camera, hands-free power liftgate
Adds bright roof rails and style upgrades
Adds 11 kW AC charger standard
Same 85 kWh battery and CCS1 port

trims.notes: AWD variant uses 67 kW front motor + 180 kW rear motor per EPA listing. Manufacturer also offers a 19 kW DC fast charger option (separate EPA entry, 288 mi range) — treated as option here.

models.notes: Equinox EV uses CCS1 port (no native NACS) for 2026 — NACS adapter offered. Two main FWD trims (LT 1, LT 2) plus RS sport trim plus AWD variants. LT 2 AWD shown above; LT 1 has no AWD option, RS AWD ~$48k. EV mirroring per §3.6 v1.1 applied to all EV trims. IIHS 2026 not on TSP list; NHTSA 5-star carries from 2025 (architecture unchanged for 2026). All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Blazer, introduced 2019; final year for current gen (8th model year) — GM reversed earlier discontinuation plans and continues the gas Blazer for 2026 with minor changes
models.model_summary: Mid-size two-row crossover, sporty-styled alongside the EV variant. Standard 2.0L turbo I4 (228 hp); 3.6L V6 (308 hp) optional on RS only. Two-row five-seat layout; FWD standard with AWD optional. Premier trim dropped for 2026.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted reliability published at research time. Earlier model years (2022-2024) received average ratings.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers like the Blazer's bold exterior, comfortable seating, and accessible V6 option on RS. Common critiques are aging interior tech (10.2-inch screen vs newer 17-inch in EV sibling), no third-row option (segment outlier), and CVT/9AT drone under load.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise styling, interior comfort, and available V6. Complaints include modest fuel economy from the 2.0T/V6 and aging cabin tech.

notable_other: 10.2-inch infotainment with wireless Apple CarPlay/Android Auto

trims.notes: 2LT FWD is the base trim with 2.0T standard. NHTSA has not yet posted 2026 ratings; IIHS has not yet awarded for 2026 (2024 'Good' ratings carry mechanically). Towing 1500 lb standard.

changes: Adds leather-appointed seating (vs cloth)
Adds Bose 8-speaker premium audio (vs 6-speaker)
Adds ventilated front seats and heated steering wheel
Adds adaptive cruise control, blind-spot monitoring, rear cross-traffic alert
Adds wireless phone charging and memory driver's seat
Same 2.0T 228 hp / 9-speed automatic / FWD

trims.notes: 3LT FWD; AWD optional. V6 not available on 3LT (RS-only).

notable_other: RS sport exterior with black accents and unique grille
RS-themed leather interior with red accents

changes: RS sport exterior styling with black accents and unique 20-inch wheels
Sport-tuned suspension
RS leather interior with red accents
8-inch driver display (vs 4.2-inch on 2LT)
Adds leather seating, Bose 8-speaker audio, ventilated front seats, heated steering wheel
Adds adaptive cruise control, blind-spot monitoring, rear cross-traffic alert
Optional 3.6L V6 (308 hp) for $1,650
Wireless phone charging and memory driver's seat

trims.notes: RS is the only trim eligible for the V6. RS AWD with V6 tops out at $49,945. 2026 drops the Premier trim.

changes: 3.6L V6 naturally aspirated 308 hp / 270 lb-ft (vs 2.0T 228 hp / 258 lb-ft)
AWD standard (vs FWD)
Towing capacity rises to 4,500 lb (vs 1,500 lb)
Fuel economy drops to 18/25/21 combined (vs 22/29/25)
All RS trim equipment (sport styling, Bose, leather, 20-inch wheels, ADAS suite)
Larger 21.7-gallon fuel tank

trims.notes: Top RS AWD V6 configuration. V6 only available on RS trim and AWD V6 is the only one with 4,500-lb towing. 2.0T AWD also available on RS, 2LT, 3LT for $2,700-$2,900 less than V6.

models.notes: Gas Blazer continued for 2026 despite earlier rumored discontinuation (GM confirmed Q4 2025). 2026 drops the Premier trim, leaving 2LT, 3LT, RS. 2.0T standard on all; V6 RS-only. AWD optional across the lineup. IIHS/NHTSA 2026 ratings not yet posted at research time (mechanically carries over from 2024 IIHS 'Good' ratings).

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Blazer EV on GM Ultium platform, introduced 2024; carryover for 2026 with refined pricing across three trims
models.model_summary: Mid-size all-electric crossover on GM Ultium platform. Three trims: LT (FWD/AWD with 85 kWh), RS (FWD/AWD with 85 kWh), and SS (AWD-only performance trim with 102 kWh battery and 615 hp). 17.7-inch touchscreen and Google Built-In standard.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports flagged early Blazer EV software issues that triggered a 2024 stop-sale. 2025-2026 software updates appear to have addressed key issues. JD Power has not yet rated 2026 MY.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Blazer EV's bold styling, large 17.7-inch screen, generous range (283-312 mi), and well-tuned ride. The SS perf trim earns enthusiast attention with 615 hp and a ~3.4 sec 0-60. Common critiques are the lack of Apple CarPlay/Android Auto (Google Built-In only), prior Ultium software reliability concerns, and CCS1 port for 2026 (NACS adapter offered).

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise styling, range, and SS performance. Complaints center on Google Built-In lock-in (no CarPlay), software glitches, and slower DC fast charging vs competition.

notable_other: One-pedal driving with Regen on Demand paddle

trims.notes: LT FWD base. Single 180 kW rear motor, 312-mi range (FWD). EV MPGe mirrored to fuel_economy fields. NHTSA 5-star from 2024 testing (architecture unchanged for 2026). IIHS not on 2026 TSP list (Acceptable on small overlap front).

changes: Adds dual-motor AWD (300 hp / 355 lb-ft) vs single-motor FWD (220 hp)
Range drops to 283 mi (vs 312 FWD); 0-60 improves to ~6.0 sec
MPGe drops to 94 combined (vs 104 FWD)
Same 85 kWh battery and equipment otherwise

trims.notes: AWD adds front motor; 67 kW front + 180 kW rear per EPA listing.

changes: RS sport exterior with unique 22-inch wheels
Adds fixed panoramic glass roof and head-up display
Adds leather seats with red contrast, ventilated front seats, memory driver's seat
Adds adaptive cruise standard; available Super Cruise hands-free
Slight range reduction (~302 mi) due to 22-inch tires (EPA ID 49645)
Same 220 hp / 180 kW rear motor / FWD

trims.notes: RS FWD uses EPA ID 49645 (22-inch wheel variant) — ~95 MPGe combined and ~302 mi range. AWD optional (300 hp / 283 mi).

changes: RS sport exterior with 22-inch wheels
Dual-motor AWD (300 hp / 355 lb-ft) standard
Panoramic glass roof, leather seats, ventilated fronts, head-up display, Super Cruise available
Range 283 mi (vs 312 FWD LT) due to AWD
Adaptive cruise standard

trims.notes: RS AWD with 22-inch wheels. EPA ID 49646.

notable_other: Wide Open Watts launch control mode
102 kWh battery with 190 kW DC fast charge peak
SS-specific 22-inch wheels with summer performance tires
Performance steering wheel with paddle shifters for regen

trims.notes: Blazer EV SS is the perf trim. Sole trim of 'ss' trim_family (singleton — own 4 image angles per spec §6.2 sole-trim rule). Treated as TRIM (not separate model) since the Chevrolet brand markets it on the single chevrolet.com/electric/blazer-ev page per the brand-note 'if own page' test (no separate SS showroom page found).

models.notes: Blazer EV LT/RS share 85 kWh battery and 220 hp FWD / 300 hp AWD. SS has its own 102 kWh battery and 615 hp AWD-only powertrain — own trim_family per perf-variant rule. CCS1 port for 2026 (NACS adapter offered). IIHS 2026 not on TSP list (Acceptable small overlap front). NHTSA 5-star carries from 2024. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen Traverse, introduced 2024 with a major redesign on GM's C1 architecture; carryover for 2026 with destination fee adjustments
models.model_summary: Three-row mid-size SUV with seating for up to 8 (LT bench) or 7 (Z71/RS/High Country captain's chairs). Single 2.5L turbo I4 powertrain (315 hp) across all trims with 8-speed automatic and FWD or AWD. Available Super Cruise hands-free driving.

reliability.summary: Current-gen Traverse is too new (2024+) for JD Power VDS (3-year window). Consumer Reports has not yet published 2026 predicted reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned Traverse for muscular styling, spacious 3-row interior, smooth ride, and feature-rich cabin (17.7-inch screen, Super Cruise available, Bose audio on upper trims). Common critiques are the loss of the V6 (now 2.5T-only) and tight 3rd-row entry vs key competitors.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise styling, interior space and tech, and Super Cruise. Complaints note the 4-cylinder's noise under load and modest fuel economy for the size class.

notable_other: Heated front seats and heated steering wheel standard

trims.notes: LT FWD is the base trim with 8-passenger bench layout. IIHS did NOT award the 2026 Traverse TSP (stricter 2026 criteria — rear-seat protection). NHTSA 5-star carries over. AWD available across all trims for ~$2,000.

notable_other: Off-road-themed Z71 styling with skid plates and unique 18-inch wheels with A/T tires
AWD standard with off-road suspension tuning
Hill descent control and drive-mode selector with terrain modes
Adaptive cruise control and adaptive headlights standard

trims.notes: Z71 is the off-road-styled trim; AWD-only. Singleton trim_family ('z71') — sole-trim rule applied (4 image angles directly on this trim).

notable_other: Sport-themed RS exterior with black accents and 22-inch wheels

trims.notes: RS is the sport-themed top-line near-tied with High Country in MSRP. AWD-standard.

notable_other: Luxury-themed High Country exterior with chrome accents and 22-inch wheels
Premium leather interior with unique stitching
Heated/ventilated front and 2nd-row seats; heated 3rd row

trims.notes: High Country is the luxury-themed top trim; AWD-standard. Singleton trim_family ('high-country').

models.notes: Traverse simplified for 2026 to LT, Z71, RS, High Country (LS base eliminated 2025). Single 2.5T powertrain across the lineup. IIHS did NOT award 2026 TSP under tighter rear-seat criteria. NHTSA 5-star carries over. All trim_families are singletons except 'lt' (LT FWD only listed; LT AWD treated as option for $2,000). Z71/RS/High Country are AWD-standard with 7-passenger captain's chairs.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Tahoe, introduced 2021; mid-cycle refresh 2024 added 17.7-inch screen and styling updates; carryover for 2026 with new 20-inch wheel design and new colors
models.model_summary: Full-size body-on-frame SUV based on Silverado 1500 chassis. Six trims (LS, LT, RST, Z71, Premier, High Country). 5.3L V8 standard; 6.2L V8 optional on RST/Z71/Premier and standard on High Country; turbo-diesel 3.0L I6 optional on all but LS. All use 10-speed automatic; RWD standard with 4WD optional (Z71 4WD-only).

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (Chevrolet Tahoe received large-SUV segment award in 2026 VDS)
reliability.summary: Chevrolet Tahoe received a JD Power 2026 Vehicle Dependability Study large-SUV segment award (one of four GM model-level wins). Chevrolet brand ranked #3 mass market at 178 PP100 in 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). However, Consumer Reports has rated recent-MY Tahoe as below-average reliability; CR 2026 ranked Chevrolet 17th overall with brand score 42/100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Tahoe's spacious cabin, strong towing/hauling capability, refreshed 17.7-inch infotainment, and excellent V8 sound from the optional 6.2L. Common critiques are below-average reliability per CR, third-row entry/exit, and high fuel consumption from gas V8s (the diesel is the only frugal option).

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise size, capability, comfort and the diesel's economy. Complaints center on tech glitches, build-quality variability and high running costs.

notable_other: 9-passenger seating with front bench (3-person front-row option)

trims.notes: Tahoe LS RWD with 9-passenger front-bench seating option. IIHS Moderate Overlap Front rated Poor (2024-2026), so no TSP/TSP+. NHTSA 5-star carries. Towing 7,900 lb standard; 8,400 lb with optional Max Trailering Package.

notable_other: Heated front seats and heated steering wheel

trims.notes: LT also available in RWD (~$2k less).

notable_other: RST sport exterior with 22-inch black alloy wheels
Adaptive cruise, blind-spot monitoring, ventilated front seats

trims.notes: RST is the street-sport themed trim.

notable_other: Off-road-themed Z71 styling with skid plates and red recovery hooks

trims.notes: Z71 is 4WD-only and the off-road-themed trim.

notable_other: Heated/ventilated front and heated 2nd-row captain's chairs (7-passenger)

trims.notes: Premier in RWD; 4WD adds ~$3,000.
High Country is the luxury flagship with 6.2L V8 standard. 4WD here; RWD ~$3k less.

models.notes: Tahoe lineup with six trims. Three engine choices (5.3L V8 / 6.2L V8 / 3.0L turbo-diesel). All use 10-speed automatic. IIHS Moderate Overlap Front rated Poor (2024-2026) — no TSP award. NHTSA 5-star carries. Z71 4WD-only; High Country 6.2L V8 standard. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: 12th-gen Suburban, introduced 2021 (shared with Tahoe); mid-cycle refresh 2024 added 17.7-inch screen; carryover for 2026 with new 20-inch wheel design and new colors
models.model_summary: Extended-length full-size SUV — Suburban shares its powertrain and trim structure with Tahoe but rides on a longer 134.1-inch wheelbase (vs Tahoe 120.9-inch) for added 3rd-row space and cargo. Same six trims (LS, LT, RST, Z71, Premier, High Country) and same engine options.

reliability.summary: Same platform as Tahoe; CR has rated current-gen Suburban below average in recent years. No 2026 specific VDS published yet.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the Suburban's class-leading 3rd-row space and cargo, refreshed 17.7-inch screen, available Super Cruise, and three engine choices including the efficient 3.0L diesel. Critiques include below-average reliability and similar gas-thirsty V8s.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise space, comfort, and capability. Complaints note build quality and tech glitches.

trims.notes: Suburban LS RWD base. Longer wheelbase than Tahoe adds 21 cu ft cargo behind 3rd row. NHTSA/IIHS 2026 ratings not specifically posted (Suburban + Tahoe share platform; Tahoe Poor on Moderate Overlap Front).
LT also offered in RWD for ~$2k less.

notable_other: Sport-themed RST exterior with 22-inch black alloys
Sport-tuned suspension and red-piped black perforated leather interior

trims.notes: RST is street-sport themed. RWD ~$3k less.

notable_other: Off-road Z71 styling with skid plates and red recovery hooks

trims.notes: Z71 4WD-only; off-road-themed.

notable_other: Polished 20-inch aluminum wheels with chrome exterior trim

trims.notes: Premier in RWD; 4WD ~$3k more.
High Country is the luxury flagship Suburban with 6.2L V8 standard.

models.notes: Suburban shares six-trim structure (LS, LT, RST, Z71, Premier, High Country) and powertrain options with Tahoe. Wheelbase 134.1 in (vs Tahoe 120.9 in) adds ~21 cu ft cargo behind 3rd row. 28-gal fuel tank (vs Tahoe 24-gal). IIHS/NHTSA 2026 ratings not specifically posted (Tahoe Poor on Moderate Overlap Front carries to platform). All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen Colorado, introduced 2023; carryover for 2026 with minor updates and pricing increases
models.model_summary: Midsize crew-cab pickup with a single 2.7L turbo I4 engine offered in three power tunes: 237 hp standard, 310 hp on LT/Trail Boss/Z71, and 310 hp HO with off-road tuning on ZR2. 8-speed automatic standard. RWD on WT/LT, 4WD standard on Trail Boss/Z71/ZR2.

reliability.summary: Current-gen Colorado received initial reliability concerns in 2023 launch year (engine and DCT issues). 2024-2026 model years show improving CR ratings. JD Power has not published 2026 VDS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise Colorado's strong turbo-4 powertrain across all trims, capable off-road ZR2/Bison variants, and modern 11.3-inch touchscreen. Common critiques are an unusually wide turning radius, modest interior materials in lower trims, and no V6 option for buyers wanting refinement over outright torque.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the turbo-4's torque, available 4WD/off-road capability, and modern infotainment. Complaints include occasional electronic glitches, transmission shift quality, and tight rear seat.

notable_other: Crew Cab with 5'2" short bed standard (only configuration for 2026)
Vinyl floor and rubberized cargo bed (WT)

trims.notes: Colorado 2026 sold only as Crew Cab with 5'2" bed. 4WD optional ~$3,300. IIHS not on 2026 TSP list (V2V front crash prevention rated Poor by IIHS). NHTSA 4-star overall (3-star rollover).

notable_other: Heated cloth seats and power driver

trims.notes: LT is the volume trim; LT 2WD ~$38,095.

notable_other: Trail Boss off-road suspension lift (~2 in)
Underbody skid plates and locking rear differential
Black exterior styling and unique grille

trims.notes: Trail Boss is the budget off-road trim (Z71-based with off-road parts).

notable_other: Z71 off-road styling with all-terrain tires on 18-inch wheels
Active off-road suspension with locking rear differential
Heated and ventilated leather front seats

trims.notes: Z71 is the comfort-oriented off-road trim with leather and Bose.

notable_other: 2.7T HO 310 hp / 430 lb-ft (highest torque in lineup)
Multimatic DSSV spool-valve dampers (3-inch front lift)
Underbody skid plates and unique fascia for approach angle
Optional ZR2 Bison package with AEV-tuned hardware ($11,700)

trims.notes: ZR2 is the hardcore off-road flagship. ZR2 Bison adds boron-steel skid plates and 35-inch Goodyear MTs.

models.notes: Colorado offered only as Crew Cab + 5'2" bed for 2026 (no Extended Cab). 2.7L turbo I4 across all trims in 237/310/310 HO outputs. ZR2 is the highest perf-off-road trim; Trail Boss is the budget off-road option. IIHS 2026 not on TSP list. NHTSA 4-star overall. ZR2 listed as TRIM (no separate chevrolet.com page) per spec §6.4 — Bison is a $11,700 package on top of ZR2 per spec §6.5.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Silverado 1500, introduced 2019; mid-cycle refresh 2022 added 13.4-inch screen; carryover for 2026 with new ZR2 Bison trim continuation and pricing adjustments
models.model_summary: Full-size half-ton pickup with five cab/bed configurations across nine trims (WT, Custom, Custom Trail Boss, LT, RST, LT Trail Boss, LTZ, ZR2, High Country). Four engines: 2.7L turbo I4 (310 hp), 5.3L V8 (355 hp), 6.2L V8 (420 hp), and 3.0L Duramax turbo-diesel I6 (305 hp). 8- or 10-speed automatic. RWD or 4WD.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports has rated recent Silverado 1500 years below average for reliability. JD Power has not published 2026 VDS yet.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise Silverado 1500's wide range of cab/bed/powertrain combinations, strong V8s, efficient Duramax diesel, and Multi-Flex tailgate availability. Common critiques are dated cabin design (refreshed but trailing Ford/Ram), below-average reliability per CR, and IIHS Poor moderate overlap front rating preventing TSP recognition.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise capability, choice of engines, and value (especially WT/Custom). Complaints include cabin tech glitches, build-quality variability, and IIHS test results.

notable_other: Regular Cab with 8'2" long bed (work-truck configuration)
Vinyl floor and rubberized cargo bed

trims.notes: WT Regular Cab Long Bed is the cheapest configuration. NHTSA 5-star for Reg/Ext Cab; Crew Cab not rated 2026. IIHS Moderate Overlap Front rated Poor — not on 2026 TSP list. Towing 9,500 lb with 2.7T.

notable_other: Crew Cab or Double Cab availability

trims.notes: Custom is the next-step lifestyle trim. Custom Trail Boss adds 4WD lift and off-road kit for ~$3,900 more.

notable_other: Heated cloth seats and heated steering wheel
Optional 6.2L V8 (420 hp) and Multi-Flex tailgate

trims.notes: LT is the volume crew-cab trim. Optional 6.2L V8 ($2,500) and Multi-Flex tailgate.

notable_other: Sport-styled RST with body-color trim and 22-inch wheels
LED headlamps and LED bed lighting

trims.notes: RST is the street-sport themed trim.

notable_other: LTZ luxury treatment with chrome accents and 20-inch wheels
Available 6.2L V8 (420 hp) and Duramax diesel

trims.notes: LTZ is the luxury-focused step. Approx $60,700 with 5.3L V8 4WD Crew Cab Short Bed.

notable_other: Multimatic DSSV spool-valve dampers with 2-inch lift
Underbody skid plates and unique high-clearance bumpers
Optional ZR2 Bison package ($11,700) with AEV-tuned components

trims.notes: ZR2 listed as TRIM here per spec §6.4 (no separate showroom page). ZR2 Bison is a package on ZR2. Top configuration with Bison ~$85,930.

notable_other: High Country luxury treatment with unique grille and chrome accents
Premium leather with massage driver's seat
Standard 5.3L V8; available 6.2L V8 or Duramax diesel

trims.notes: High Country is the luxury flagship Silverado 1500. Approx $67,995 with 5.3L V8 4WD Crew Cab Short Bed. Tops out at $71,800 with 6.2L.

models.notes: Silverado 1500 lineup nine trims (WT, Custom, Custom Trail Boss, LT, RST, LT Trail Boss, LTZ, ZR2, High Country) across Regular/Double/Crew cabs and three bed lengths. Four engines (2.7T, 5.3L V8, 6.2L V8, 3.0L diesel). 8AT or 10AT. Custom Trail Boss and LT Trail Boss represented in lineup but not split into separate trim entries here (they are 4WD-lift variants of Custom and LT). IIHS NOT on 2026 TSP list (Moderate Overlap Front: Poor). NHTSA 5-star (Reg/Ext Cab; Crew not rated). All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Silverado HD, introduced 2020; mid-cycle refresh 2024 added 13.4-inch screen; carryover for 2026 with new 20-inch wheel design and pricing increases
models.model_summary: Heavy-duty pickup line marketed by Chevrolet as one model spanning 2500HD (3/4-ton) and 3500HD (1-ton) GVWRs. Six trims (WT, Custom, LT, LTZ, ZR2, High Country). Two engines: 6.6L gas V8 (401 hp) standard with 8-speed automatic, or Duramax 6.6L turbo-diesel V8 (470 hp / 975 lb-ft) with Allison 10-speed automatic. Trailering up to 36,000 lb gooseneck (3500HD with diesel and dual rear wheels).

reliability.summary: Heavy-duty trucks have limited sample sizes in JD Power VDS and Consumer Reports. No 2026 model-specific scores published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Silverado HD's class-leading 36,000-lb max gooseneck towing, refined Duramax/Allison powertrain, comfortable cabin with 13.4-inch screen, and available Super Cruise even with trailers. Critiques include weight and length make daily driving challenging, and option pricing pushes top trims beyond $90k.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise capability, comfort, and the Duramax. Complaints note size, fuel consumption with the gas V8, and DEF maintenance for diesel models.

notable_other: Vinyl floor and 6-passenger bench/captain's chairs options
Optional 6.6L Duramax turbo-diesel V8 (470 hp / 975 lb-ft) with Allison 10-speed

trims.notes: Silverado 2500HD WT 2WD. Powertrain warranty extends to 5yr/100k on HD trucks. No EPA fuel economy published (HD trucks above 8500 lb GVWR are exempt from EPA testing). No NHTSA/IIHS rating (similarly exempt for heavy-duty trucks). Towing 17,000 lb conventional with gas V8 (Max Trailering Package).

notable_other: Heated cloth seats and heated steering wheel
Wireless phone charging and power driver's seat

trims.notes: LT 4WD is the volume Crew Cab trim. RWD also available.

notable_other: Towing 22,420 lb conventional with Max Trailering Package
Heated/ventilated leather seats with memory driver
Available Super Cruise (including hands-free with trailer)

trims.notes: LTZ 4WD with Duramax is the volume diesel configuration.

notable_other: Multimatic DSSV spool-valve dampers with 1.5-inch lift
Modified front fascia for off-road approach angle
Optional ZR2 Bison package with AEV-tuned upgrades

trims.notes: ZR2 HD trim — off-road HD truck. Bison package on top adds $11,700+.

notable_other: High Country luxury treatment with unique chrome grille and 20-inch chrome wheels

trims.notes: High Country 4WD Duramax — luxury flagship HD. Diesel adds ~$10.5k; gas High Country starts ~$76,395.

models.notes: Silverado HD marketed as one model with 2500HD (3/4-ton) and 3500HD (1-ton) GVWRs. 3500HD has higher gooseneck (32,000-36,000 lb) and dual-rear-wheel option. Six trims (WT, Custom, LT, LTZ, ZR2, High Country). Two engines (6.6L gas / 6.6L Duramax diesel). No EPA MPG ratings (>8500 lb GVWR exempt). No NHTSA/IIHS ratings (HD trucks not crash-tested). All images flagged needs_scraping. Trims here represent Crew Cab Standard Bed; 3500HD adds Long Bed and DRW options at price premiums.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Silverado EV on GM Ultium platform, introduced 2024 (Fleet WT first, then RST). For 2026: RST trim sunsetted, new Trail Boss off-road trim added, three trims (WT, LT, Trail Boss) with three battery options each
models.model_summary: Full-size all-electric pickup on GM Ultium platform with three trims (WT, LT, Trail Boss) and three battery sizes each (Standard ~286 mi, Extended ~410 mi, Max ~493 mi). Dual-motor AWD-electric standard with up to 645 hp / 765 lb-ft on LT. 4WD-standard, Crew Cab + 5'11" bed only.

reliability.summary: Silverado EV is new (2024+ launch) so no JD Power VDS yet. Consumer Reports has not yet published 2026 predicted reliability; early production saw software-related delivery delays in 2024.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Silverado EV's class-leading 493-mile Max Range option, 645 hp on LT trims, innovative MidGate for 10'10" cargo capacity, and capable 12,500-lb towing. Common critiques are heavy curb weight (~8,500-9,200 lb), CCS1 port for 2026, no proper third-row option, and the WT's lower 510 hp tune.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise range, MidGate flexibility, and performance. Complaints include weight, slow public DC charging vs Tesla/Rivian, and high MSRP for top trims.

notable_other: MidGate not available on WT (separates bed/cab on LT/Trail Boss)

trims.notes: WT Standard Range is the base trim. 4WD-standard. WT trims have 14-module battery (Standard, ~130 kWh net assumed) and use dual ACPM motors (255+256 kW). Extended Range WT goes to $68,295 / ~424 mi; Max Range WT $76,295 / 493 mi (segment-leading). NHTSA/IIHS 2026 ratings not specifically posted for Silverado EV.

notable_other: 645 hp / 765 lb-ft (highest output across truck lineup)
MidGate fold-flat opens to 10'10" cargo length
Fixed glass roof and heated/ventilated leather seats

trims.notes: LT is the volume trim with MidGate and 645 hp. Extended Range LT $71,195 / 410 mi; Max Range LT $91,295 / 478 mi.

notable_other: Off-road Trail Boss styling with red recovery hooks
Adaptive air suspension with 2-inch lift (10-inch ground clearance)
Wide Open Watts launch mode bumps to 625 hp / 775 lb-ft

trims.notes: Trail Boss is new for 2026 — first electric Silverado off-road trim. Trail Boss Max Range $88,695. EPA ID 49638 (20-module battery, 19kW 6-mode charger).

models.notes: Silverado EV simplified to three trims for 2026 with RST sunsetted. Trail Boss is new — first electric Silverado off-road trim with 35-inch tires and air suspension. Each trim available in Standard/Extended/Max Range. CCS1 port (NACS adapter offered). Max Range WT achieves 493 miles — class-leading. NHTSA/IIHS 2026 ratings not specifically posted for Silverado EV. EV warranty 8yr/100k. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: 8th-gen (C8) Corvette Stingray, introduced 2020 as the first mid-engine production Corvette; carryover for 2026 with destination fee increase ($1,995 to $2,495 mid-year)
models.model_summary: Mid-engine 2-seat sports car with 6.2L naturally-aspirated V8 (490 hp / 465 lb-ft) and 8-speed dual-clutch automatic. Offered as Coupe (Targa removable roof) or Convertible (retractable hardtop). Three equipment levels: 1LT (base), 2LT, 3LT.

reliability.summary: C8 Corvette has small volume in JD Power VDS and CR predicted reliability sampling. Early-gen quality issues (2020-2021) have largely resolved.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers universally praise the Stingray's value-per-performance ratio at $70k (sub-3-sec 0-60 with Z51), exotic mid-engine layout, and 490 hp naturally-aspirated V8. Common critiques are limited cargo space, harsh ride with Z51, and dual-clutch low-speed jerkiness.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise performance, looks, and value vs European exotics. Complaints note tight cargo and DCT low-speed behavior.

notable_other: Z51 Performance Package optional (+50 hp, magnetic ride, performance brakes)

trims.notes: Stingray base Coupe with removable Targa panel. 2.9 sec 0-60 with Z51 optional. 2LT $79,095 adds heated/ventilated seats, HUD, performance data recorder. 3LT $83,745 adds GT2/Competition seats, full leather, Bose surround. No NHTSA/IIHS testing for Corvette.
Convertible has its own trim_family. 2LT/3LT Convertibles available at $86,095 and $90,745 respectively.

models.notes: Corvette Stingray treated as separate model from Z06/ZR1/E-Ray/ZR1X per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule (each has own chevrolet.com showroom page). Coupe and Convertible split as separate trim_families per body-style rule. 2LT and 3LT equipment tiers treated as packages within each body style per §6.5 (only base 1LT shown for each body). EPA ID 49170. No NHTSA/IIHS testing for Corvette. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: C8 Corvette E-Ray, introduced 2024 as the first hybrid Corvette and first AWD Corvette; carryover for 2026
models.model_summary: Hybrid AWD Corvette pairing the 6.2L LT2 V8 (495 hp) with a 160-hp front-axle electric motor for combined 655 hp. eAWD-electric drivetrain with 8-speed dual-clutch. Sub-3-sec 0-60. Offered as Coupe and Convertible.

reliability.summary: E-Ray volume is low; no JD Power VDS or CR predicted reliability data yet for 2026.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the E-Ray's drama-free all-weather AWD traction, brutal 2.5-sec 0-60, and stealth electric-only mode for short crawls. Critiques are extra weight from hybrid hardware and modest 1-mile electric-only range (parking-lot use only).

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise all-weather usability and acceleration. Complaints note pricing premium over Stingray and complexity of hybrid system.

notable_other: Stealth Mode (electric-only crawl up to 45 mph for ~1 mile)
Charge+ mode lets engine top up the battery

trims.notes: E-Ray treats hybrid as 'hybrid' not 'phev' — 1.9 kWh battery non-plug-in. Convertible variant available $115,600 (1LZ Cabrio). Stealth Mode is electric-only crawl, not extended EV range. Sole-trim of 'coupe' family within E-Ray model. NHTSA/IIHS not tested.
Convertible body variant. Sole-trim of 'convertible' family within E-Ray model.

models.notes: E-Ray is the AWD hybrid C8 Corvette. EPA ID 49774. Treated as separate model per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule (own chevrolet.com showroom page). Hybrid type (not PHEV) — non-plug-in 1.9 kWh battery. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: C8 Corvette Z06, introduced 2023; track-focused mid-engine flagship with naturally-aspirated 5.5L flat-plane-crank V8; carryover for 2026
models.model_summary: Track-focused Corvette with 670-hp flat-plane-crank 5.5L LT6 V8 — the highest-revving NA V8 in any production car (8,600 rpm redline). 8-speed dual-clutch automatic; RWD. Available widebody Z07 performance package. Coupe and Convertible bodies.

reliability.summary: Low volume; no JD Power VDS or CR predicted reliability published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Z06 as the most thrilling NA V8 sports car at any price, with Ferrari-rivaling sound and razor-sharp track handling. Critiques are road harshness with Z07 carbon-ceramic brakes and limited daily driver practicality.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the V8 sound and on-track performance. Complaints note ride comfort and dealer markups.

notable_other: 5.5L LT6 flat-plane-crank NA V8 (670 hp, 8,600 rpm redline)
Optional Z07 Performance Package (~$10k): carbon-fiber wheels, carbon-ceramic brakes, FE7 suspension, Michelin Sport Cup 2R tires
'Carbon Aero' aero kit available with Z07

trims.notes: Z06 1LZ Coupe base. Convertible $124,700 (1LZ Cabrio). Z07 Carbon Aero EPA ID 49173 (14 MPG). 'Gas Guzzler' tax applies. Sole-trim of 'coupe' family within Z06 model.
Z06 Convertible body. Sole-trim of 'convertible' family. 3LZ Convertible $138,250.

models.notes: Z06 is the track-focused NA V8 Corvette. EPA ID 49172 standard / 49173 with Carbon Aero. Treated as separate model per perf-variant rule. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: C8 Corvette ZR1, introduced 2025 as the ultimate ICE Corvette with twin-turbo 5.5L LT7 V8; carryover for 2026
models.model_summary: Ultimate ICE Corvette: 1,064-hp twin-turbo 5.5L LT7 V8 (highest output ever from a US-built production V8) with 233 mph top speed. 8-speed DCT, RWD only. Available widebody Z07 Performance Package. Coupe and Convertible bodies.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new/low-volume for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Corvette ZR1 is too new and low-volume for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports sampling. Brand-level: Chevrolet ranked #3 mass market at 178 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked Chevrolet 17th overall with brand score 42/100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); model is also too new/low-volume for typical APEAL coverage.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers call the ZR1 a hypercar-killer at one-third the price — sub-9-minute Nurburgring time projected, 233-mph top speed, and instantaneous twin-turbo response. Critiques are the long order backlog and dealer markups well above MSRP.

owner_reviews.summary: Too few owner reviews published yet for the 2025/2026 ZR1.

notable_other: Optional Z07 package adds rear wing, carbon-fiber wheels, Michelin Sport Cup 2R tires

trims.notes: ZR1 Coupe base. 'Gas Guzzler' tax applies. EPA ID 49171. Convertible $189,000.
ZR1 Convertible. Sole-trim of 'convertible' family within ZR1 model.

models.notes: ZR1 is the twin-turbo flagship of ICE Corvette lineup at 1,064 hp. EPA ID 49171. Treated as separate model per perf-variant rule. All images flagged needs_scraping.

models.generation_context: All-new for 2026: ZR1X combines ZR1's twin-turbo LT7 V8 with E-Ray's front-axle hybrid motor for a 1,250-hp electrified AWD Corvette
models.model_summary: Electrified AWD Corvette — combines ZR1's 1,064-hp twin-turbo LT7 V8 (rear axle) with E-Ray's 186-hp front-axle motor for 1,250 hp combined, sub-2-sec 0-60. 8-speed DCT to rear axle, single-speed to front. Coupe and Convertible. New for 2026.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Corvette ZR1X is brand-new for 2026 — too recent for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports sampling. Brand-level: Chevrolet ranked #3 mass market at 178 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). CR 2026 ranked Chevrolet 17th overall with brand score 42/100.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); model is also too new/low-volume for typical APEAL coverage.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers call ZR1X the most accelerative production car on sale at any price (sub-2-sec 0-60, mid-7-sec 1/4 mile), with all-weather AWD usability that no other 1,000+ hp car offers. Critiques are limited dealer availability and 5-figure markups.

owner_reviews.summary: Too few owner reviews published yet for the new 2026 ZR1X.

notable_other: 5.5L LT7 twin-turbo V8 (rear) + 186 hp front e-motor — 1,250 hp combined
Carbon-fiber widebody and carbon-ceramic brakes standard
Optional ZTK Performance Package (~$10k) with active aero
Quail Silver Limited Edition (Convertible 3LZ only, 2026 production start)

trims.notes: ZR1X 1LZ Coupe AWD. Includes $1,995 destination at MSRP pricing per GM press release. 3LZ Coupe $218,395; 3LZ Convertible $228,395; Quail Silver Limited Edition 3LZ Convertible $241,395. Sole-trim of 'coupe' family within ZR1X model. EPA ID 50271. Hybrid type (non-plug-in 1.9 kWh battery shared with E-Ray) per spec §3.4.
ZR1X Convertible. Sole-trim of 'convertible' family within ZR1X model. 3LZ Convertible $228,395; Quail Silver Limited 3LZ Cabrio $241,395.

models.notes: ZR1X is all-new for 2026 — pairs ZR1's twin-turbo LT7 with E-Ray's front-axle e-motor for 1,250 hp AWD. EPA ID 50271. Hybrid type (non-plug-in). Treated as separate model per perf-variant rule. Coupe and Convertible split per body-style rule. Quail Silver Limited Edition (Convertible 3LZ) production starts 2026. All images flagged needs_scraping.
